info.title: LabelGrid API
info.description:
# **LabelGrid API Documentation**

LabelGrid API provides comprehensive endpoints for content providers to manage their audio catalogs, releases, distribution, and analytics. This RESTful API supports catalog management, track and release operations, distribution workflows, and integrations with external music services.


## Prerequisites

The following prerequisites are required to use the LabelGrid API:

- You must have an active LabelGrid account
- API features enabled on your account
- IP allowlisting (if using sandbox environment)

## Authorization
All endpoints require authentication via API tokens. Include your token in the `Authorization` header as a Bearer token. You may generate and manage your API tokens in your [LabelGrid account settings](https://app.labelgrid.com/user/profile/api-tokens) or [Sandbox account settings](https://frontend-sandbox.stg.labelgrid.com/user/profile/api-tokens).

> NOTICE
> For experimenting and testing purposes, we highly recommend using the sandbox environment. See the "Sandbox Access" section below for details.

## Sandbox Access

### Sandbox IP Allowlisting

Contact support to allowlist any IP addresses you will be using to access the sandbox resources.

> NOTE
> Allowlisting is only needed for sandbox access.

### Sandbox API

You can use the following sandbox version of our API to create and manage resources without affecting your production data.

- [API Sandbox](https://api-sandbox.stg.labelgrid.com/docs/api)

### Sandbox Frontend

You can use the following sandbox version of our frontend to check your catalogue after creating resources via the API.


- [Frontend Sandbox](https://frontend-sandbox.stg.labelgrid.com/login)


## Keeping Your Data Secure

> IMPORTANT
> LabelGrid takes data security seriously. Ensure you follow best practices for API key management.

- Never expose your API keys in public repositories or client-side code.
- Only use your key within server-side code to keep your keys secure.
- Do not share your API keys with unauthorized parties.
- Never hard-code API keys in your applications.
- Use environment variables or secure vaults to store sensitive information.
- Regularly rotate your API keys and monitor their usage.

## Features Overview
- **Catalog Management**: Create, update, and delete artists, labels, publishers, and writers.
- **Release Operations**: Manage releases, including track listings, metadata, and cover art.
- **Distribution Workflows**: Submit releases for distribution to various music services and platforms.

For more information, visit our [LabelGrid Help Center](https://labelgrid.com/contact/) or contact our support team.

## API Changelog

Track breaking changes, deprecations, additions, and fixes to the Public API:

- HTML: [/changelog](https://api.labelgrid.com/changelog)
- Atom feed: [/changelog.atom](https://api.labelgrid.com/changelog.atom)

Authenticated public-API responses also advertise the feed via an RFC 8288 `Link: </changelog.atom>; rel="changelog"` header.

page. Idempotent — an existing short URL is reused, not duplicated.

delete.description: Permanently delete a release from your catalog. This action cannot be undone. Note: Releases that have been distributed may have restrictions on deletion based on business rules.

post.description: Upload or replace the release's cover art image.

Validate a release for pre-release requirements including artists, tracks, metadata, and business rules.

`errors` are human-readable strings. `errors_structured` is an additive,
machine-readable sibling with one entry per string (1:1, same order): each
carries a stable `code`, the `entity_type` it concerns (`release`, `track`,
`artwork`, or `audio`), the `entity_id` it attaches to (nullable), and the
public payload `field` name where one applies (nullable). Checks that
cannot be attributed emit `code: "GENERAL"` with `entity_type: "release"`
rather than being dropped.

`warnings` / `warnings_structured` are a parallel, **non-blocking** advisory
channel in the same shape. A non-empty `warnings` never affects `result` —
the release still validates OK; the submitter is informed but not gated.

This endpoint is for submitting your release for distribution. Processing will begin as soon as your request is reviewed by our team. ⚠️ To configure or change your DSP's use the release update endpoint with the `dsp_configs` field.

- The release must be in a valid state to be distributed.
- The release will be reviewed before any distribution occurs.
- While the release is under review critical fields cannot be edited or resubmitted.

Issues a managed takedown of the release from all eligible outlets. A Delete/Purge message is queued to each store the release was delivered to, and the release's distribution intent is recorded as a takedown.

- Only the release owner may take down the release.
- A release under a blocking/infringement lock cannot be taken down.
- Owner semantics: the release is not rejected, so you may redistribute later without re-approval.

get.description:
Preflight QC is an optional add-on for API distribution accounts: once
automated checks finish, a submitted release is held for your own
quality check — review the results, fix what you want, then confirm the
release into distribution review.

Returns the customer-facing issues found by the automated checks plus
report metadata (`generated_at`, `checks_in_progress`, `stale`, `hold`,
`review_status`, `release_status`, and the report profile name/version).
Issues are populated only while the release is held with checks
complete. `stale` is `true` when the release was edited after
`generated_at` (the held findings no longer reflect the current release):
re-run the analysis with the refresh endpoint, then confirm.

Requires Preflight QC to be enabled for the owning account
(`403 pre_review_qc_not_enabled` otherwise — contact our sales team to
enable it).
get.summary: Get the Preflight QC report for a release

post.description:
Editing a held release no longer re-runs the automated checks — it only
marks the report stale. Call this endpoint to explicitly start a fresh
analysis cycle once you have finished editing: the checks re-run, the
`release.preflight.report_ready` webhook fires when they complete, and the
report's `stale` flag clears.

The call is idempotent while a cycle is already running: it returns the
in-progress state without queuing a second analysis (and without spending
budget). A per-release hourly budget applies to cycles actually started —
exceeding it returns `429 preflight_recheck_limit_reached` with a
`Retry-After` header.

Conflict responses: `409 not_in_customer_review_hold` (the release is not
awaiting your review), `409 refresh_coalesced` (an analysis just
completed moments ago — retry after the `Retry-After` seconds). The
release must pass validation first (`403 RELEASE_NOT_VALIDATED`), and
Preflight QC must be enabled for the owning account
(`403 pre_review_qc_not_enabled`).
post.summary: Re-run the Preflight QC analysis for a held release

post.description:
Part of Preflight QC (optional add-on). Confirms a release currently
held for your quality check, moving it into LabelGrid's distribution
review — the same routing that would have applied without the hold.
Supports `Idempotency-Key` replay: a retry with the same key returns
the original result without confirming twice.

Conflict responses: `409 not_in_customer_review_hold`,
`409 checks_in_progress`, `409 release_not_in_review`,
`409 checks_stale` (the release was edited after the analysis
completed — re-run it via the quality-report refresh endpoint first).
Requires Preflight QC to be enabled for the owning account
(`403 pre_review_qc_not_enabled`).
post.summary: Confirm a held release into distribution review

post.description:
The deliberate inverse of submit-for-review: while a release is still
awaiting review, its owner can pull it back to draft so they can edit and
resubmit — instead of waiting for the review to be approved or sent back.
The release and its active review move from review back to draft, and the
submission lock is released, so every field is editable again.

Conflict response: `409 RELEASE_NOT_UNDER_REVIEW` when the release is not
currently awaiting review (already approved, rejected, sent back, or a
draft). Pairs with submit-for-review.

get.description:
Read a release's landing-page (link & pre-save) configuration: the
tri-state `links_page_enabled` gate, whether the release inherits the
label default or carries its own custom config, the resolved effective
config (actions, CTA, description, pre-order links, page style), and the
preview URL.

put.description:
Enable/disable and configure a release's landing page. Sets the tri-state
`links_page_enabled` gate and, transactionally, either inherits the label
default (`config_mode=inherit` drops the release's own row) or upserts a
custom config (actions, CTA text, description, pre-order links, page
style). Invalid values are rejected with 422 and nothing is persisted.

get.description:
Retrieve the list of user-visible review issues for a release.
Issues are only returned when the release is in a state where the customer
is the actor: `require_changes` or `rejected`. In all other states (draft,
to_review, approved) the endpoint returns an empty array to avoid surfacing
partially-evaluated issues mid-review.

Returns the issues visible on your release's review.

Each issue carries its full conversation thread under `notes` — customer
notes (via POST /review-issues/{reviewReleaseIssue}/notes) and operator
replies in chronological order. `notes` is an empty array when none have
been submitted.
get.summary: Get review issues for a release

post.description:
Attach a user-supplied note to a review release issue that is open for
customer response. Each POST APPENDS a new note to the issue's conversation
thread — prior notes are preserved, each keeping its own LLM verdict. The
returned id identifies the newly-created note.

Notes can only be submitted when the release is in a state where the
customer is the actor (`require_changes` or `draft`), the underlying
issue is `confirmed`, and the issue definition allows user notes.

Authorization: the caller must be able to update the release that owns
this review issue. Notes can be added only to issues visible on the
release's active review; anything else returns 404.
post.summary: Submit a note on a review issue

get.description:
Returns the customer-facing issue definitions for the active quality-report
profile, keyed by their public string code (slug), each with its curated
customer-safe title and message plus severity, is_blocking, requires_feedback
and type, alongside the report profile name/version. Requires Preflight QC to
be enabled for the authenticated account (`403 pre_review_qc_not_enabled`).

Fail-closed as a WHOLE: `503 issue_definitions_unavailable` when there is no
active quality-report profile, or when ANY of its codes lacks a curated
customer-safe layer or a clean public slug — never a partial catalog.

post.description:
Generate a temporary presigned URL for direct file upload.

**Workflow:**
1. Get presigned URL (this endpoint)
2. Upload the file to the presigned URL using a PUT request
3. Create track file record with the returned key

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific track file.

put.description:
Upload a new file for a track. If a file of the same asset type already exists, it will be replaced. Files are processed asynchronously and made available for distribution upon successful processing.

**For stereo/dolby (choose one):**
1. Presigned upload: `{ "s3_key": "uuid/song.wav" }` - File is queued for async processing (202 Accepted)
2. Remote URL: `{ "source_url": "https://...", "checksum": "sha256" }` - File is downloaded, then queued for transcoding (201 Created)

**Supported audio formats:** WAV (16-, 24-, or 32-bit) or FLAC (16-bit only).
Hi-res FLAC sources (>16-bit) are rejected with 422 — upload WAV for hi-res delivery.

**For lyrics (LRC only):**
- Presigned upload: `{ "s3_key": "uuid/lyrics.lrc" }` - LRC file is queued for processing (202 Accepted). Only `.lrc` format via `s3_key` is supported.

**Note:** File is queued for processing. Check status via GET endpoint.

message.const: File queued for processing. Check status via GET endpoint.
LRC file queued for processing. Check status via GET endpoint.

delete.description: Delete a track file from storage and database. For stereo/dolby, this is permanent. For lyrics, the synced LRC file is removed but untimed TTML may be regenerated from plain text lyrics if available.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all licenses for a specific track, including both cover and sample licenses.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page (default: 50, max: 100).

post.description: Upload a license file for a track. This endpoint handles direct file upload and creates the license record.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific track license.

post.description: Update the metadata and/or file for a specific track license. Can replace the file and update metadata.

delete.description: Permanently delete a track license and its associated file. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: Generate a temporary download URL for a track license file. The URL expires after 20 minutes.
Retrieve a paginated list of all tracks accessible to user, including their associated release, label, artists, and genres.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page (default: 50, max: 100).

parameters.name: filter[isrc]
parameters.description: Filter tracks by ISRC. Accepts dashes or no dashes (e.g. `US-ABC-21-12345` or `USABC2112345`). Case-insensitive exact match.

data.description: The list of items

release_id.description: The ID of the release this track belongs to

mix_versions.description: Do not include this field if this track does not have a mix version or sub-title

disc.description: Only include this if this a multi-disc release

anyOf.description: Was the artwork created with AI assistance?
Was the composition or lyrics created with AI assistance?

recording_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code where the track was recorded (e.g. US, GB)

audio_language.description:
BCP-47 locale code (e.g. en, es-419, fr, ja-Jpan, ko).
Use zxx if there are no words in the audio track

preferred_localization.description: BCP-47 locale code, e.g. en, es-419, fr, ja-Jpan, ko - Spotify and a few other DSP's only allow 1 language per product in their automatic ingestion system, so this field serves as a way for you to determine which Title and Artist language variation gets sent to them for this product. Default is always English

anyOf.description: Does the track contain commercial samples?

dolby_isrc.description: Do not include this if you are not uploading a dolby version of the track. Leave blank to auto-generate. Note: The Dolby ISRC should be a unique code different from the standard ISRC

cline.description: Use cline_year + cline_name. Combined cline is emitted during a 3-month grace period, then removed.

pline.description: Use pline_year + pline_name. Combined pline is emitted during a 3-month grace period, then removed.

instant_gratification.description:
Instant Gratification Setting
If true, this track will be available as an instant grat track for pre-orders

release_date.description:
Track-level original release date. Optional — when omitted the track
inherits the release-level date. Set this for a simple per-track date
override (advanced "Enable advanced release date control" off)

release_pre_order_date.description:
Track-level pre-order date. Optional. When both are present it must be
before release_date

default_display_artist.description: Auto-generated display artist inferred from the artists array

remixer.description: Auto-generated remixer inferred from the artists array

title.description: Auto-generated track title based from the preferred localization in the titles array

mix_version.description: Auto-generated mix version based from the preferred localization in the mix_versions array

display_title.description: Auto-generated display title combining title and mix version

audio_preview_url.description: Auto-generated audio preview URL inferred from the track's master file

path.description: Base path for paginator generated URLs.

per_page.description: Number of items shown per page.

to.description: Number of the last item in the slice.

total.description: Total number of items being paginated.

post.description: Create a new track in your catalog. The track must be associated with a release that belongs to your label.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific track, including its release, label, artists, and genres.

parameters.name: track
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the track

delete.description: Permanently delete a track from your catalog. This action cannot be undone.

post.description:
Generate a temporary presigned URL for direct file upload.

**Workflow:**
1. Get presigned URL (this endpoint)
2. Upload the file to the presigned URL using a PUT request
3. Create release file record with the returned key

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific release file.

put.description:
Upload a new file for a release. If a file of the same asset type already exists, it will be replaced.
Files are processed securely and made available for distribution upon successful upload.

delete.description: Permanently delete a release file from both storage and database. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all active and non-deprecated distribution outlets, including their supported genres and takedown settings.
Retrieve a paginated list of distribution queue jobs with optional filters for release ID, outlet ID, and status.

data.description: The list of items

path.description: Base path for paginator generated URLs.

per_page.description: Number of items shown per page.

to.description: Number of the last item in the slice.

total.description: Total number of items being paginated.

get.description: Retrieve site settings configuration for a specific label, including theme, branding, and display preferences.
Retrieve all configured social media, streaming, and retail platform links for a specific label, organized by platform type.
Resolve a label using its slug or site group ID and generate a temporary API token for public access.
Retrieve detailed information about the currently authenticated user, including their statistics.
Retrieve a paginated list of all webhooks configured by the authenticated user.

data.description: The list of items

path.description: Base path for paginator generated URLs.

per_page.description: Number of items shown per page.

to.description: Number of the last item in the slice.

total.description: Total number of items being paginated.

post.description:
Create a new webhook endpoint to receive event notifications.
Maximum 10 webhooks per user. URL must use HTTPS.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all available webhook event types, each with the
schema of the inner `data` payload it delivers (field name => type).
Every release-scoped event includes `release_id` and `label_id`;
`payment.statement_ready` is account-scoped and includes neither.

enum: Triggered when a release is successfully delivered to an outlet
Triggered when delivery to an outlet fails
Triggered when a takedown request completes
Triggered when a release review status changes
Triggered when the Preflight QC report for a held release is ready to fetch
Triggered when a release is distributed
Triggered when a payment statement is ready for viewing
Triggered when a track's audio transcode completes successfully
Triggered when a track's audio transcode fails or ends incomplete
Triggered on every per-outlet distribution queue status transition
Triggered when Stream Radar publishes a new or reopened streaming-integrity flag for the account
Triggered when a Stream Radar flag resolves

review_issues.description:
Conditionally emitted: present only on transitions where the
customer is the next actor (require_changes / rejected) and
absent (key omitted) otherwise. Marked optional so consumers
do not treat it as always-present.
review_issues.const: array (optional; present only on require_changes/rejected transitions)

generated_at.description:
When the automated checks completed (ISO 8601) — matches the
quality-report endpoint's report.generated_at.

profile.description:
The report profile the counts were computed through — matches
the quality-report endpoint's report.profile.

counts.description:
Aggregate counts ONLY — the payload never carries the issues
themselves; fetch the full report from the quality-report
endpoint. requires_feedback counts issues that need written
feedback before they can clear (it overlaps the other two).

files.description:
Per-file detail for the track: the asset type id and its current
TrackFileStatus value.

previous_status.description:
The prior DistroQueueStatusEnum value; null when the row had no
recognised previous status.

transition.description:
Which write produced this event; also the dedupe key. 'reopened'
fires when a previously resolved flag re-qualifies.
transition.const: string ('published'|'reopened' — dedupe key; 'reopened' fires when a previously resolved flag re-qualifies)

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific webhook.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a webhook. This action cannot be undone.
All associated delivery logs will also be deleted.

post.description:
Send a test event to the webhook endpoint to verify it's working correctly.
This will send a test payload and return the response status.

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of delivery attempts for a specific webhook.
Logs are retained for 30 days.

data.description: The list of items

path.description: Base path for paginator generated URLs.

per_page.description: Number of items shown per page.

to.description: Number of the last item in the slice.

total.description: Total number of items being paginated.

post.description:
Generate a new secret key for HMAC signature verification.
The old secret will be invalidated immediately.

delete.description:
Revoke the personal access token presented on this request (self-revoke).
The same token stops authenticating on its very next request. Returns
`422 NOT_REVOCABLE` when the acting credential is not a revocable
API token.

message.const: This credential cannot be revoked through this endpoint.

delete.description:
Revoke one of the caller's own `public_api` tokens by numeric id. The
operation is idempotent — revoking an already-revoked owned token returns
`200`. To avoid disclosing which token ids exist, a token that is
not found, not owned by the caller, or non-revocable all return an
identical `404`.

message.const: This credential may not use the token-revoke API.

404.description: Identical response for not-found / not-owned / sites-token — no existence leak.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all active music genres with their categories and base genre information.
Retrieve a list of all available contributor roles that can be assigned to contributors and writers.
Retrieve a list of all active music genre categories available for classification.

Retrieve a list of all available royalty statements for the authenticated user, ordered by period descending. Each statement includes a `periods_covered` array that indicates which actual sales periods are included in the payment, which may differ from the payment period when payments have been skipped or unclaimed in certain months. It is normal for multiple statements to cover overlapping periods if payments were delayed or reissued.

Note: The `labelgrid_fee` field shows the fee rate that was in effect when this statement was generated. Actual fees applied to individual line items may differ from this reference value. Refer to the detailed CSV export or transactions ledger for precise fee calculations per line item.

Opt-in per-label filtering: pass any of `filter[label_id]`, `filter[release_id]`, `filter[isrc]` or `filter[upc]` to switch to server-recomputed per-label gross-revenue totals sourced from the royalty line-item layer (grouped by payment period). The filtered rows carry a `scope` of `label`, a single monetary `gross_usd`, and a `meta.gross_definition` discriminator; all account-level-only fields (`amount`, `opening_balance`, tax/vat/transfer, `total_due_usd`, `invoice_number`, `date_paid`) are `null`. `gross_usd` matches the customer-facing UI + CSV column of the same name: gross revenue AFTER the statement-provider fee (e.g. Merlin/Orchard) and BEFORE LabelGrid platform/UGC fees; net revenue (after LabelGrid fees) and the final payout are on this statements endpoint (unfiltered). `filter[start_date]`/`filter[end_date]` (Y-m-d) narrow by payment period and require an entity filter. Only labels you directly own are visible; an unowned `label_id` returns `404` and an unowned `upc`/`isrc` returns an empty `data` array. With no filter the response is unchanged.

This endpoint returns one of two shapes depending on whether an entity filter is present. The `@response` schema below is the union of both — filtered-only keys (`scope`, `filter`, `gross_usd`) and the top-level `meta` block are present only in filtered mode, and every account-level field is nullable so a filtered row (where those fields are `null`) validates against the same schema.

- **Unfiltered** (no `filter[*]` entity): full account-level statement rows. `invoice_number`, `period`, `status`, `currency`, `created_at` are strings; `opening_balance`, `amount`, `labelgrid_rate`, `labelgrid_fee`, `total_due_usd` are floats; there is no top-level `meta`.
- **Filtered** (any `filter[*]` entity present): server-recomputed per-label gross-revenue rows, one per payment period. Each row carries `scope: "label"`, the echoed `filter`, `period`, `currency`, and a single monetary `gross_usd` (gross revenue after the statement-provider fee, before LabelGrid platform/UGC fees — matching the customer-facing UI + CSV `gross_usd` column). All account-level-only fields (`invoice_number`, `status`, `invoice`, `ref`, `opening_balance`, `amount`, `labelgrid_rate`, `labelgrid_fee`, the tax/vat/transfer fields, `total_due_usd`, `date_paid`, `created_at`) are `null`. A top-level `meta` block adds `scope`, the echoed `filter`, `gross_definition: "post_provider_fee_pre_labelgrid_fee"`, and a `note` (net revenue after LabelGrid fees and the final payout are on the unfiltered statements endpoint). Consumers branch on `scope`; the filtered shape is not a strict superset of the unfiltered one.

Per-release rollup: add `group_by=release` (which **requires** `filter[label_id]`) to get one row per release under that label, grouped by payment period, instead of one per-label total. Amounts not attributable to a specific release (and any per-release rounding residue) surface as an explicit remainder row with `release_id: null` per period — never silently apportioned or dropped — so the per-release rows plus the remainder always sum exactly to the per-label total the non-grouped path returns for the same filters. Grouped rows carry `scope: "release"` and a `release_id` (null on the remainder row); `meta` adds `group_by: "release"`. Any other `group_by` value, or `group_by=release` without `filter[label_id]`, returns `422`.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description:
Opt-in per-release rollup. Closed allow-list (only "release") so an
arbitrary / injection-shaped value is a 422, never a SQL grouping key.

parameters.name: filter[label_id]
parameters.description: Recompute per-label gross_usd totals (gross revenue after the statement-provider fee, before LabelGrid fees) for a label you own.

parameters.name: filter[release_id]
parameters.description: Recompute per-label gross_usd totals narrowed to a release you own.

parameters.name: filter[isrc]
parameters.description: Recompute per-label gross_usd totals narrowed to a track ISRC.

parameters.name: filter[upc]
parameters.description: Recompute per-label gross_usd totals narrowed to a release UPC.

parameters.name: filter[start_date]
parameters.description: Lower payment-period bound (Y-m-d). Requires an entity filter.

parameters.name: filter[end_date]
parameters.description: Upper payment-period bound (Y-m-d). Requires an entity filter.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific royalty statement by its invoice number.
Request a CSV export of detailed line items for a specific statement by invoice number. The export is processed asynchronously and cached for 24 hours.

Request a CSV export of detailed line items for a specific statement period. The export is processed asynchronously and cached for 24 hours. Type can be "month" (e.g., 2025-01) or "year" (e.g., 2025).

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: The start date for the export range. @var string @example 2025-01-01

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: The end date for the export range. @var string @example 2025-01-31

get.description: Download the invoice PDF for a specific statement by invoice number.

Retrieve a paginated list of accounting transactions for the authenticated user's account, ordered by created_at descending (most recent first).

Opt-in per-label filtering: pass any of `filter[label_id]`, `filter[release_id]`, `filter[isrc]` or `filter[upc]` to switch to server-recomputed per-label `REVENUE` rows sourced from the royalty line-item layer (one row per payment period). Filtered rows carry a `scope` of `label`, `transaction_type` of `REVENUE`, a single monetary `gross_usd`, and a `meta.gross_definition` discriminator; ledger `balance_before`/`balance_after` are `null`. `gross_usd` matches the customer-facing UI + CSV column of the same name: gross revenue AFTER the statement-provider fee (e.g. Merlin/Orchard) and BEFORE LabelGrid platform/UGC fees; net revenue (after LabelGrid fees) and the final payout are on the statements endpoint. `filter[start_date]`/`filter[end_date]` (Y-m-d) narrow by payment period and require an entity filter. Only labels you directly own are visible; an unowned `label_id`/`release_id` returns `404` and an unowned `upc`/`isrc` returns an empty `data` array. With no filter the response is unchanged.

This endpoint returns one of two shapes depending on whether an entity filter is present. The `@response` schema below is the union of both — filtered-only keys (`scope`, `period`, `gross_usd`) and the filtered `meta` fields (`scope`, `filter`, `gross_definition`, `note`) are present only in filtered mode, and every ledger field is nullable so a filtered row (where those fields are `null`) validates against the same schema.

- **Unfiltered** (no `filter[*]` entity): paginated account-level ledger rows. `id` is an int; `transaction_date`, `transaction_type`, `created_at` are strings; `net_usd`, `balance_before`, `balance_after` are floats; `links`/`meta` are the standard pagination envelope.
- **Filtered** (any `filter[*]` entity present): server-recomputed per-label `REVENUE` rows, one per payment period, returned in the same paginated envelope (which degrades to a single page). Each row carries `scope: "label"`, `transaction_type: "REVENUE"`, `period`, `currency`, and a single monetary `gross_usd` (gross revenue after the statement-provider fee, before LabelGrid platform/UGC fees — matching the customer-facing UI + CSV `gross_usd` column). All account-level-only fields (`id`, `transaction_date`, `reference_number`, `vendor`, `vendor_statement_id`, `category`, `net_usd`, `balance_before`, `balance_after`, `description`, `created_at`) are `null`, and `links` are all `null`. `meta` additionally carries `scope`, the echoed `filter`, `gross_definition: "post_provider_fee_pre_labelgrid_fee"`, and a `note` (net revenue after LabelGrid fees and the final payout are on the statements endpoint). Consumers branch on `scope`; the filtered shape is not a strict superset of the unfiltered one.

Per-release rollup: add `group_by=release` (which **requires** `filter[label_id]`) to get one `REVENUE` row per release under that label, grouped by payment period, instead of one per-label total. Amounts not attributable to a specific release (and any per-release rounding residue) surface as an explicit remainder row with `release_id: null` per period — never silently apportioned or dropped — so the per-release rows plus the remainder always sum exactly to the per-label total the non-grouped path returns for the same filters. Grouped rows carry `scope: "release"` and a `release_id` (null on the remainder row); `meta` adds `group_by: "release"`. Any other `group_by` value, or `group_by=release` without `filter[label_id]`, returns `422`.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description:
Opt-in per-release rollup. Closed allow-list (only "release") so an
arbitrary / injection-shaped value is a 422, never a SQL grouping key.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page (default: 500, max: 3000).

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order by created date. Accepts `asc` or `desc` (default: `desc`).

parameters.description: Recompute per-label REVENUE rows narrowed to a release UPC.

get.description:
Retrieve the per-release/track breakdown of the Spotify artificial-streaming
fee for one reporting period (`YYYY-MM`) on the authenticated user's account.
On a statement the fee shows as one aggregated `ARTIFICIAL_FEE` line that
carries a `period`; this endpoint returns the underlying rows for that period
so the customer can attribute the fee. Lines are ordered by amount, largest
first, and sum to `total_amount`.

Return royalties for the authenticated user's owned labels, grouped by a caller-chosen
ordered set of dimensions (`group_by`), decomposing the same provider-fee-adjusted
`gross_usd` figure the statements/transactions filter path and the customer CSV use.
This is the JSON equivalent of the async CSV line-item export's per-track × per-DSP ×
per-territory granularity, without downloading and parsing a file.

**`group_by` is required** and is an ordered, comma-separated, distinct subset of
`track,dsp,release,territory,period` (e.g. `group_by=track,dsp`). It is a closed
allow-list — any other token, a duplicate, or an empty token returns `422` and never
reaches the query. Each row carries only the keys for the requested dimensions
(`isrc`/`dsp`/`release_id`/`territory`/`period`, each possibly `null` when the value is
unattributable), plus `currency` and a single monetary `gross_usd`. A line with a blank
retailer under a known track surfaces as `{ "isrc": "…", "dsp": null }` — known
attribution is never discarded. The terminal page carries one synthetic rounding-
adjustment row so the sum of every row exactly equals `meta.total_gross_usd`; that row
has every dimension key `null` AND an additional `adjustment: true` key. Only the
adjustment row carries `adjustment` — that key, not "all dimensions null", distinguishes
it from a genuine all-null-dimension group (a real blank-retailer group under
`group_by=dsp` is also `{ "dsp": null, … }` but has no `adjustment` key).

**Snapshot-backed pagination.** The grouped result is aggregated once, at a single
instant, and frozen — every page (and every cursor read) is served from that snapshot,
so per-group totals are self-consistent across the whole walk. Page forward with the
opaque `cursor` from `links.next` until it is `null`. A cursor is bound to its snapshot,
user, and exact query params; an invalid, expired, foreign, or mismatched cursor returns
`422` — restart without a cursor for a fresh snapshot. `meta.as_of` is the snapshot
instant and `meta.snapshot_expires_at` when it (and its cursors) expire.

`gross_usd` is gross revenue after the statement-provider fee (e.g. Merlin/Orchard) and
before LabelGrid platform/UGC fees — matching the customer-facing UI + CSV column of the
same name. Net revenue (after LabelGrid fees) and the final payout remain on the
statements endpoint. Only labels you directly own are visible; an unowned
`filter[label_id]`/`filter[release_id]` returns `404` and a foreign `upc`/`isrc` returns
empty `data`. `dsp` and `territory` are the raw DSP-reported values (parity with the
CSV), not server-normalized.

parameters.name: group_by
filter[label_id]
parameters.description: Scope the breakdown to a label you own.

parameters.name: filter[release_id]
parameters.description: Scope the breakdown to a release you own.

parameters.name: filter[start_date]
parameters.description: Lower payment-period bound (Y-m-d). Requires an entity filter.

parameters.name: filter[end_date]
parameters.description: Upper payment-period bound (Y-m-d). Requires an entity filter.

get.description:
Return the authenticated user's own artificial-stream DETECTIONS across
Spotify and Apple as a single, newest-first, cursor-paginated collection —
one row per detected track/period, including per-country detail where the
source reports it. This is the detection-level companion to
`GET /artificial-streaming-fee/{period}`, which exposes only the resulting
fee: `quantity` here is the count of detected artificial streams; the money
lives on the fee endpoint.

**Source asymmetry.** `country` is Spotify-only and is ALWAYS `null` for
Apple detections (Apple's report carries no country). `upc` and
`penalty_fee_suspended` are likewise Spotify-only (`null` for Apple). Rows
carry a `dsp` discriminator (`spotify` | `apple`) so consumers can branch.

**Date rule.** Only detections with a report date (`date_reported`,
`YYYY-MM-DD`) are returned; Spotify's report date is nullable and any
null-date row is excluded (the cursor keys on the report date).

**Pagination.** Forward-only opaque `cursor` from `links.next`; page until it
is `null`. The cursor is a signed, stateless keyset token bound to your
account scope and the exact filter set — it never expires, but a tampered
token or a token replayed after you change the scope/filters returns `422`
(restart without a cursor). Because detections are append-only historical
rows keyed by report date, a late import whose report date sorts BEFORE a
boundary you have already paged past is only picked up on a fresh pull (start
a new listing without a cursor).

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description:
Bounds mirror the sibling accounting reads (config-driven, same
default/max as the royalties/breakdown endpoint) so partners reuse
one paging loop.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all instruments.

Returns all 15 metric sections — or, when a non-empty `metrics[]` selector
is supplied, just that subset — in a single response, plus a `meta` block.

The `metrics[]` parameter is OPTIONAL. Omitting it entirely AND supplying
it as an EMPTY array (`metrics[]=` with no values) BOTH return all 15
sections — an empty selector is never treated as "zero sections". Only a
non-empty `metrics[]` narrows the response to the listed section keys.

data.description: The analytics data, keyed by section (each section is the same array the matching standalone endpoint returns as its `data`).

meta.description: Query metadata for the summary (resolved date range, platform, scope, selected sections, and generation time).

availability.description: Per-section availability for the selected platform, keyed by section. Present only when a platform filter is applied; each value indicates whether that metric is reported for the platform.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all languages.

Retrieve a paginated list of the authenticated account's Stream Radar
flags — the anomalous-streaming alerts raised on your releases. Filter by
status, severity, DSP, ISRC, release, and the last-detected date range.
Results are ordered by most recent detection.

Requires Stream Radar to be enabled for the account
(`403 stream_radar_not_enabled` otherwise).

parameters.name: filter[detected_from]
parameters.description: Only flags last detected on or after this date (Y-m-d).

parameters.name: filter[detected_to]
parameters.description: Only flags last detected on or before this date (Y-m-d).

get.description:
Retrieve a single Stream Radar flag owned by the authenticated account.
Flags belonging to other accounts return `404` (no existence leak).

Requires Stream Radar to be enabled for the account
(`403 stream_radar_not_enabled` otherwise).
Retrieve a list of all territories.

items.description: A genre item is a name string (<=255 chars) or a numeric id.

FlagSeverity.description: Severity band for a published Stream Radar flag: one of low, medium, high.
FlagSeverity.title: FlagSeverity

FlagStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a published Stream Radar flag. A flag reopens back to ACTIVE on a later qualifying detection, so RESOLVED is not terminal.
FlagStatus.title: FlagStatus

PublicReleaseDate.description:
Public-API shape of a release date, used for both the create/update request
and the release response.

Mirrors the per-region date fields. Set `exclude: true` on a date set to
exclude those `countries` from delivery (territory exclusion — "release
 * worldwide except X, Y"). The flag round-trips faithfully on read.

countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes (e.g. "US", "GB") or "WW" for worldwide

exclude.description:
Territory exclusion flag. Set `true` to exclude this `countries` set
from delivery — "release worldwide except these territories". Requires
`countries`; the per-set date sub-fields are ignored when `true`.
Echoed back on read so the release_dates array round-trips

validity_period_end.description:
The date (or date-time) this territory set stops delivering — a
scheduled per-territory takedown. Set a FUTURE value on a delivering
(non-`exclude`) set to keep the release live now and end it on that
date; the value is emitted verbatim as the delivery window's end. Distinct
from `exclude: true`, which is an immediate territory exclusion. Must be
on or after `validity_period_start`; `end == start` (a closed window) is
valid. A value in the PAST is rejected with `422` on create; on update
it is rejected too, unless every country in the set already carries that
exact same persisted end (so echoing a naturally-elapsed end returned by
GET back unchanged is accepted). Omit for an open-ended window

PublicReleaseDate.title: PublicReleaseDate

artists.description: Note: Updating this field replaces all existing artists. Do not include this field to keep existing artists

mix_versions.description: Do not include this field if this track does not have a mix version or sub-title

cat.description: Your preferred unique catalog number for this release

release_dates.description: Advanced release dates - only use this if you wish to override the original release date

artwork_ai_usage.description: Was the artwork created with AI assistance?

preferred_localization.description: BCP-47 locale code, e.g. en, es_419, fr, ja_Jpan, ko

cline.description: Use cline_year + cline_name. Combined cline is accepted during a 3-month grace period, then removed.

pline.description: Use pline_year + pline_name. Combined pline is accepted during a 3-month grace period, then removed.

transfer_from_distributor.description: Name of the previous distributor when this release is a transfer/migration from another distributor

dsp_configs.description: Use 'all_dsps' to change all dsp's. Note: Updating this field replaces all existing configurations. Do not include this field to keep existing configurations

enable_exact_release_time.description:
Deliver the exact release time (GMT) instead of flooring the release
date to midnight (T00:00:00Z). When true, the time component of the
release date is honoured in the delivered store metadata; a midnight
time with the flag set is legal and delivers midnight exactly. Omit
the field (or send false) to keep the date-only, floored behaviour

default_display_artist.description: Auto-generated display artist for this release. This field is automatically inferred from the artists array

distro_outlet_id.description: Distro outlet slug e.g. spotify, applemusic, or numeric outlet id

rights_claim_behavior.description: Content Rights Claim Policy: 0 = Allow (default), 1 = Block

exclusive_period.description:
Beatport exclusivity window. Enum: "2" | "4" | "8" | "lifetime". Null = non-exclusive.
Only honored for outlets where `supports_exclusive_period = true` (Beatport)

public-visibility), so the owner sees whether the page is live.

commercial_samples.description: Does the track contain commercial samples?

audio_language.description:
BCP-47 locale code (e.g. en, es-419, fr, ja-Jpan, ko).
Required on create. Use zxx if there are no words in the audio track

preferred_localization.description: BCP-47 locale code, e.g. en, es-419, fr, ja-Jpan, ko - Spotify and a few other DSP's only allow 1 language per product in their automatic ingestion system, so this field serves as a way for you to determine which Title and Artist language variation gets sent to them for this product. Default is always English

dolby_isrc.description: Do not include this if you are not uploading a dolby version of the track. Leave blank to auto-generate. Note: The Dolby ISRC should be a unique code different from the standard ISRC

cline.description: Use cline_year + cline_name. Combined cline is accepted during a 3-month grace period, then removed.

pline.description: Use pline_year + pline_name. Combined pline is accepted during a 3-month grace period, then removed.

release-level date. Set this for a simple per-track date override
instead of the advanced per-territory release_dates array

before release_date

master_splits.description:
Master Splits
Splits between label and artists

release_date.description:
Track-level original release date. Optional — omit to leave unchanged,
send null to clear (track then inherits the release-level date). Set
this for a simple per-track date override instead of the advanced
per-territory release_dates array

release_pre_order_date.description:
Track-level pre-order date. Optional. When both are present it must be
before release_date

release_dates.description: Advanced track-level release dates - Only set this if you wish to override the release-level dates

config_mode.description:
Customize vs inherit: 'inherit' deletes the release config row so it
falls back to the label default; 'custom' (or omitted) upserts it.

tracks_remaining.description:
Derived convenience: remaining headroom, or null when the
limit is unlimited (-1 sentinel / null). null means
"unlimited", NOT "0 left".

release_submission_limit.description:
Matches the enforcement applied when you submit a release for
distribution. null when no cap applies.

terms_acceptance.description:
Non-blocking Terms-of-Service re-acceptance signal. A ToS bump does
NOT block API writes; this lets a consumer surface an "action
 required" banner and points the account holder at the web
acceptance page. Informational only — it never gates a request.
